```{r}
pgs_desc_height <- pgs_desc_height[pgs_desc_height$label %in% c('QuickPRS','COJO','p+t: 5e-08'),]

# 1. Reshape the data for faceting
# We move 'mean' and 'sd' into a single 'metric' column
pgs_long <- pgs_desc_height %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(mean, sd), 
               names_to = "metric", 
               values_to = "value")

# 2. Create the plot
ggplot(pgs_long, aes(x = gwas, y = value, fill = type)) +
  # Use stat = "identity" to plot the actual values in the table
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) +
  # Facet rows by 'metric' (mean/sd) and columns by 'pop' (EUR/TRANS)
  facet_grid(metric ~ pop + label, scales = "free") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Polygenic Score Summary Statistics",
       x = "GWAS Source",
       y = "Value",
       fill = "Type") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Plot selected param across traits
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

pgs_desc$label <- gsub('.*_0_|.*_quickprs|.*_external', '', pgs_desc$param)
pgs_desc$label[pgs_desc$label != '']<-paste0('p+t: ', pgs_desc$label[pgs_desc$label != ''])
pgs_desc$label[pgs_desc$method == 'quickprs']<-'QuickPRS'
pgs_desc$label[grepl('cojo', pgs_desc$gwas)]<-'COJO'
pgs_desc$label<-factor(pgs_desc$label, levels = unique(pgs_desc$label))

# Retain p+t with 5e-8 only
pgs_desc <- pgs_desc[pgs_desc$label %in% c('QuickPRS','COJO','p+t: 5e-08'),]

# 1. Reshape the data for faceting
# We move 'mean' and 'sd' into a single 'metric' column
pgs_long <- pgs_desc %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(mean, sd), 
               names_to = "metric", 
               values_to = "value")

# 2. Create the plot
ggplot(pgs_long, aes(x = gwas, y = value, fill = type)) +
  # Use stat = "identity" to plot the actual values in the table
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) +
  # Facet rows by 'metric' (mean/sd) and columns by 'pop' (EUR/TRANS)
  facet_grid(metric ~ pop, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Polygenic Score Summary Statistics",
       x = "GWAS Source",
       y = "Value",
       fill = "Type") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

```

***

## Check mean and sd of PCs

```{r}
setwd('/users/k1806347/oliverpainfel/Software/MyGit/GenoPred/pipeline/')
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(cowplot)

source('../functions/misc.R')
source_all('../functions')

# Set config
config <- 'misc/opensnp/config_zenodo_10715132.yaml'
gwas_list <- fread('misc/opensnp/gwas_list_zenodo_10715132.txt')

# Target PCs
target_ref_projected_pc_dt <- read_pcs(config = config, name = 'opensnp', type = 'projected')

# Print mean PGS using GenoPred corrections
pc_desc <- NULL
for(i in names(target_ref_projected_pc_dt$opensnp)){
  for(j in names(target_ref_projected_pc_dt$opensnp[[i]])[-1:-2]){
    pc_desc <- rbind(
      pc_desc,
      data.table(
        pop = i,
        pc = j,
        mean = mean(target_ref_projected_pc_dt$opensnp[[i]][[j]]),
        sd = sd(target_ref_projected_pc_dt$opensnp[[i]][[j]])
      ))
  }
}

library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Reshape the data for faceting
# We move 'mean' and 'sd' into a single 'metric' column
pcs_long <- pc_desc %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(mean, sd), 
               names_to = "metric", 
               values_to = "value")

# 2. Create the plot
ggplot(pcs_long, aes(x = pc, y = value, fill = pc)) +
  # Use stat = "identity" to plot the actual values in the table
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) +
  # Facet rows by 'metric' (mean/sd) and columns by 'pop' (EUR/TRANS)
  facet_grid(metric ~ pop, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "PC Summary Statistics",
       x = "PC",
       y = "Value",
       fill = NULL) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

```

## Check PC association with PGS

### Create functions

```{r}
library(data.table)
library(FNN)
library(glm2)

###############################################################################
# Helper functions
###############################################################################

get_pgs_columns <- function(dt, gwas = NULL, method = NULL, pgs_col = NULL) {
  dt <- as.data.table(dt)
  
  if (!all(c("FID", "IID") %in% names(dt))) {
    stop("PGS data must contain FID and IID.")
  }
  
  # If user specifies one or more PGS columns, use them
  if (!is.null(pgs_col)) {
    missing <- setdiff(pgs_col, names(dt))
    if (length(missing) > 0) {
      stop("Specified pgs_col not found: ", paste(missing, collapse = ", "))
    }
    return(pgs_col)
  }
  
  exclude_cols <- c("FID", "IID")
  candidate_cols <- setdiff(names(dt), exclude_cols)
  
  # Keep numeric columns only
  numeric_cols <- candidate_cols[sapply(dt[, ..candidate_cols], is.numeric)]
  
  if (length(numeric_cols) == 0) {
    stop("No numeric PGS columns found after excluding FID/IID.")
  }
  
  # Prefer columns matching GWAS name if possible, but allow multiple matches
  if (!is.null(gwas)) {
    gwas_matches <- numeric_cols[grepl(gwas, numeric_cols, fixed = TRUE)]
    if (length(gwas_matches) > 0) {
      return(gwas_matches)
    }
  }
  
  # Otherwise return all numeric score columns
  return(numeric_cols)
}

prep_pgs_dt <- function(pgs_dt, gwas = NULL, method = NULL, pgs_col = NULL) {
  dt <- as.data.table(copy(pgs_dt))
  
  if (!all(c("FID", "IID") %in% names(dt))) {
    stop("PGS data must contain FID and IID.")
  }
  
  score_cols <- get_pgs_columns(
    dt = dt,
    gwas = gwas,
    method = method,
    pgs_col = pgs_col
  )
  
  dt <- dt[, c("FID", "IID", score_cols), with = FALSE]
  
  for (col in score_cols) {
    dt[, (col) := as.numeric(get(col))]
  }
  
  return(list(
    dt = dt,
    score_cols = score_cols
  ))
}


prep_pc_dt <- function(pc_dt, pc_cols) {
  dt <- as.data.table(copy(pc_dt))
  
  if (!all(c("FID", "IID") %in% names(dt))) {
    stop("PC data must contain FID and IID.")
  }
  
  if (!all(pc_cols %in% names(dt))) {
    missing_cols <- setdiff(pc_cols, names(dt))
    stop("Missing PC columns: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
  }
  
  dt <- dt[, c("FID", "IID", pc_cols), with = FALSE]
  return(dt)
}


get_pc_matrices <- function(ref_dt, target_dt, pc_cols) {
  # PCs are assumed to already be on the same reference-projected scale:
  # reference mean approximately 0, reference SD approximately 1,
  # target projected and scaled using the same reference parameters.
  ref_mat <- as.matrix(ref_dt[, ..pc_cols])
  target_mat <- as.matrix(target_dt[, ..pc_cols])
  
  return(list(
    ref = ref_mat,
    target = target_mat
  ))
}


summarise_score <- function(x) {
  n <- sum(!is.na(x))
  x_mean <- mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  x_sd <- sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  x_se <- x_sd / sqrt(n)
  
  data.table(
    mean = x_mean,
    sd = x_sd,
    se = x_se,
    t = x_mean / x_se,
    p = t.test(x, mu = 0)$p.value
  )
}


check_projected_pc_scaling <- function(ref_pc_dt, target_pc_dt, pc_cols) {
  ref_dt <- as.data.table(ref_pc_dt)
  target_dt <- as.data.table(target_pc_dt)
  
  ref_summary <- data.table(
    PC = pc_cols,
    ref_mean = sapply(pc_cols, function(pc) mean(ref_dt[[pc]], na.rm = TRUE)),
    ref_sd = sapply(pc_cols, function(pc) sd(ref_dt[[pc]], na.rm = TRUE))
  )
  
  target_summary <- data.table(
    PC = pc_cols,
    target_mean = sapply(pc_cols, function(pc) mean(target_dt[[pc]], na.rm = TRUE)),
    target_sd = sapply(pc_cols, function(pc) sd(target_dt[[pc]], na.rm = TRUE))
  )
  
  merge(ref_summary, target_summary, by = "PC")
}


###############################################################################
# Linear PC correction equivalent to existing TRANS correction
###############################################################################

linear_pc_correction_trans_equivalent <- function(ref_dt, target_dt, pc_cols) {
  mean_form <- as.formula(
    paste("PGS ~", paste(pc_cols, collapse = " + "))
  )
  
  mean_model <- lm(mean_form, data = ref_dt)
  
  ref_mu <- predict(mean_model, newdata = ref_dt)
  target_mu <- predict(mean_model, newdata = target_dt)
  
  ref_resid <- ref_dt$PGS - ref_mu
  target_resid <- target_dt$PGS - target_mu
  
  ref_var_dt <- copy(ref_dt[, ..pc_cols])
  ref_var_dt[, squared_residuals := pmax(ref_resid^2, 1e-4)]
  
  var_form <- as.formula(
    paste("squared_residuals ~", paste(pc_cols, collapse = " + "))
  )
  
  var_model <- glm2(
    var_form,
    data = ref_var_dt,
    family = Gamma(link = "log")
  )
  
  ref_var_hat <- exp(predict(var_model, newdata = ref_dt))
  target_var_hat <- exp(predict(var_model, newdata = target_dt))
  
  ref_trans <- ref_resid / sqrt(ref_var_hat)
  target_trans <- target_resid / sqrt(target_var_hat)
  
  return(list(
    mean_model = mean_model,
    var_model = var_model,
    
    ref_mu = ref_mu,
    target_mu = target_mu,
    
    ref_var_hat = ref_var_hat,
    target_var_hat = target_var_hat,
    
    ref_resid = ref_resid,
    target_resid = target_resid,
    
    ref_trans = ref_trans,
    target_trans = target_trans
  ))
}


###############################################################################
# KNN correction
###############################################################################

knn_expected_pgs <- function(ref_pgs,
                             ref_pc,
                             target_pc,
                             k = 50,
                             weighting = c("inverse_distance", "uniform"),
                             eps = 1e-8) {
  weighting <- match.arg(weighting)
  
  if (k > nrow(ref_pc)) {
    stop("k is larger than number of reference individuals.")
  }
  
  knn <- FNN::get.knnx(
    data = ref_pc,
    query = target_pc,
    k = k
  )
  
  idx <- knn$nn.index
  dist <- knn$nn.dist
  
  pgs_mat <- matrix(
    ref_pgs[idx],
    nrow = nrow(idx),
    ncol = ncol(idx)
  )
  
  if (weighting == "uniform") {
    w <- matrix(1 / k, nrow = nrow(idx), ncol = k)
  } else {
    w <- 1 / (dist + eps)
    w <- w / rowSums(w)
  }
  
  mu_hat <- rowSums(pgs_mat * w)
  
  var_hat <- rowSums(w * sweep(pgs_mat, 1, mu_hat, "-")^2)
  sd_hat <- sqrt(var_hat)
  sd_hat[sd_hat == 0 | is.na(sd_hat)] <- NA_real_
  
  n_eff <- 1 / rowSums(w^2)
  
  return(list(
    mu_hat = mu_hat,
    sd_hat = sd_hat,
    idx = idx,
    dist = dist,
    weights = w,
    n_eff = n_eff,
    mean_dist = rowMeans(dist),
    min_dist = dist[, 1]
  ))
}


knn_reference_leave_one_out <- function(ref_pgs,
                                        ref_pc,
                                        k = 50,
                                        weighting = c("inverse_distance", "uniform"),
                                        eps = 1e-8) {
  weighting <- match.arg(weighting)
  
  if ((k + 1) > nrow(ref_pc)) {
    stop("k + 1 is larger than number of reference individuals.")
  }
  
  knn <- FNN::get.knnx(
    data = ref_pc,
    query = ref_pc,
    k = k + 1
  )
  
  idx <- knn$nn.index[, -1, drop = FALSE]
  dist <- knn$nn.dist[, -1, drop = FALSE]
  
  pgs_mat <- matrix(
    ref_pgs[idx],
    nrow = nrow(idx),
    ncol = ncol(idx)
  )
  
  if (weighting == "uniform") {
    w <- matrix(1 / k, nrow = nrow(idx), ncol = k)
  } else {
    w <- 1 / (dist + eps)
    w <- w / rowSums(w)
  }
  
  mu_hat <- rowSums(pgs_mat * w)
  var_hat <- rowSums(w * sweep(pgs_mat, 1, mu_hat, "-")^2)
  sd_hat <- sqrt(var_hat)
  sd_hat[sd_hat == 0 | is.na(sd_hat)] <- NA_real_
  
  resid <- ref_pgs - mu_hat
  z <- resid / sd_hat
  
  return(list(
    mu_hat = mu_hat,
    sd_hat = sd_hat,
    resid = resid,
    z = z,
    idx = idx,
    dist = dist,
    weights = w,
    n_eff = 1 / rowSums(w^2),
    mean_dist = rowMeans(dist),
    min_dist = dist[, 1]
  ))
}

tune_knn_k_reference <- function(
    ref_dt,
    pc_cols,
    k_values = c(10, 20, 50, 100, 200),
    weighting = "inverse_distance"
) {
  pc_mat <- as.matrix(ref_dt[, ..pc_cols])
  
  out <- rbindlist(lapply(k_values, function(k_i) {
    
    loo <- knn_reference_leave_one_out(
      ref_pgs = ref_dt$PGS,
      ref_pc = pc_mat,
      k = k_i,
      weighting = weighting
    )
    
    resid <- loo$resid
    z <- loo$z
    
    # Residual association with PCs
    tmp <- data.table(
      z = z,
      resid = resid,
      ref_dt[, ..pc_cols]
    )
    
    tmp <- tmp[complete.cases(tmp)]
    
    fit_resid <- lm(
      as.formula(paste("resid ~", paste(pc_cols, collapse = " + "))),
      data = tmp
    )
    
    fit_z <- lm(
      as.formula(paste("z ~", paste(pc_cols, collapse = " + "))),
      data = tmp
    )
    
    data.table(
      k = k_i,
      n_ref = nrow(ref_dt),
      
      resid_mean = mean(resid, na.rm = TRUE),
      resid_sd = sd(resid, na.rm = TRUE),
      resid_rmse = sqrt(mean(resid^2, na.rm = TRUE)),
      resid_pc_r2 = summary(fit_resid)$r.squared,
      
      z_mean = mean(z, na.rm = TRUE),
      z_sd = sd(z, na.rm = TRUE),
      z_pc_r2 = summary(fit_z)$r.squared,
      
      mean_n_eff = mean(loo$n_eff, na.rm = TRUE),
      median_n_eff = median(loo$n_eff, na.rm = TRUE),
      mean_dist = mean(loo$mean_dist, na.rm = TRUE),
      median_dist = median(loo$mean_dist, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  }), fill = TRUE)
  
  # Simple combined score.
  # Lower is better.
  out[, criterion := 
        abs(z_mean) +
        abs(log(z_sd)) +
        z_pc_r2 +
        resid_pc_r2
  ]
  
  out[order(criterion)]
}

############
# Discrete correction
############

discrete_reference_correction <- function(ref_pgs, target_pgs) {
  ref_mean <- mean(ref_pgs, na.rm = TRUE)
  ref_sd <- sd(ref_pgs, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  if (is.na(ref_sd) || ref_sd == 0) {
    stop("Reference PGS SD is zero or missing; cannot perform discrete correction.")
  }
  
  ref_discrete <- (ref_pgs - ref_mean) / ref_sd
  target_discrete <- (target_pgs - ref_mean) / ref_sd
  
  return(list(
    ref_mean = ref_mean,
    ref_sd = ref_sd,
    ref_discrete = ref_discrete,
    target_discrete = target_discrete
  ))
}

###############################################################################
# Main analysis function for one GWAS-method combination
###############################################################################

analyse_one_score_col_simple <- function(
    ref_score_dt,
    target_score_dt,
    ref_pc_dt,
    target_pc_dt,
    score_col,
    gwas,
    method,
    pc_cols = paste0("PC", 1:20),
    k_knn = 50,
    weighting = "inverse_distance"
) {
  message("Analysing: ", gwas, " / ", method, " / ", score_col)
  
  ref_pgs <- ref_score_dt[, .(
    FID,
    IID,
    PGS = as.numeric(get(score_col))
  )]
  
  target_pgs <- target_score_dt[, .(
    FID,
    IID,
    PGS = as.numeric(get(score_col))
  )]
  
  ref_pgs <- ref_pgs[!is.na(PGS)]
  target_pgs <- target_pgs[!is.na(PGS)]
  
  # Merge PGS with reference-projected PCs
  ref_dt <- merge(
    ref_pgs,
    prep_pc_dt(ref_pc_dt, pc_cols),
    by = c("FID", "IID"),
    all = FALSE
  )
  
  target_dt <- merge(
    target_pgs,
    prep_pc_dt(target_pc_dt, pc_cols),
    by = c("FID", "IID"),
    all = FALSE
  )
  
  if (nrow(ref_dt) == 0) {
    stop("No overlap between reference PGS and reference PCs for ", score_col)
  }
  
  if (nrow(target_dt) == 0) {
    stop("No overlap between target PGS and target PCs for ", score_col)
  }
  
  ref_dt <- ref_dt[
    complete.cases(ref_dt[, c("PGS", pc_cols), with = FALSE])
  ]
  
  target_dt <- target_dt[
    complete.cases(target_dt[, c("PGS", pc_cols), with = FALSE])
  ]
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Raw PGS
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  
  target_dt[, pgs_raw := PGS]
  ref_dt[, pgs_raw := PGS]
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Discrete ancestry correction:
  # standardise target PGS using mean and SD of ancestry-matched reference group
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  
  discrete_fit <- discrete_reference_correction(
    ref_pgs = ref_dt$PGS,
    target_pgs = target_dt$PGS
  )
  
  ref_dt[, pgs_discrete := discrete_fit$ref_discrete]
  target_dt[, pgs_discrete := discrete_fit$target_discrete]
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Linear TRANS-equivalent correction
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  
  linear_fit <- linear_pc_correction_trans_equivalent(
    ref_dt = ref_dt,
    target_dt = target_dt,
    pc_cols = pc_cols
  )
  
  ref_dt[, linear_mu := linear_fit$ref_mu]
  ref_dt[, linear_var := linear_fit$ref_var_hat]
  ref_dt[, pgs_linear_resid := linear_fit$ref_resid]
  ref_dt[, pgs_linear := linear_fit$ref_trans]
  
  target_dt[, linear_mu := linear_fit$target_mu]
  target_dt[, linear_var := linear_fit$target_var_hat]
  target_dt[, pgs_linear_resid := linear_fit$target_resid]
  target_dt[, pgs_linear := linear_fit$target_trans]
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # KNN correction using already-scaled reference-projected PCs
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  
  pc_mats <- get_pc_matrices(
    ref_dt = ref_dt,
    target_dt = target_dt,
    pc_cols = pc_cols
  )
  
  target_knn <- knn_expected_pgs(
    ref_pgs = ref_dt$PGS,
    ref_pc = pc_mats$ref,
    target_pc = pc_mats$target,
    k = k_knn,
    weighting = weighting
  )
  
  target_dt[, knn_mu := target_knn$mu_hat]
  target_dt[, knn_sd := target_knn$sd_hat]
  target_dt[, pgs_knn_resid := PGS - knn_mu]
  target_dt[, pgs_knn := pgs_knn_resid / knn_sd]
  target_dt[, knn_n_eff := target_knn$n_eff]
  target_dt[, knn_mean_dist := target_knn$mean_dist]
  target_dt[, knn_min_dist := target_knn$min_dist]
  
  # Reference leave-one-out KNN correction
  ref_knn <- knn_reference_leave_one_out(
    ref_pgs = ref_dt$PGS,
    ref_pc = pc_mats$ref,
    k = k_knn,
    weighting = weighting
  )
  
  ref_dt[, knn_mu_loo := ref_knn$mu_hat]
  ref_dt[, knn_sd_loo := ref_knn$sd_hat]
  ref_dt[, pgs_knn_resid_loo := ref_knn$resid]
  ref_dt[, pgs_knn_loo := ref_knn$z]
  ref_dt[, knn_n_eff_loo := ref_knn$n_eff]
  ref_dt[, knn_mean_dist_loo := ref_knn$mean_dist]
  ref_dt[, knn_min_dist_loo := ref_knn$min_dist]
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Summary
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  
  raw_sum <- summarise_score(target_dt$pgs_raw)
  discrete_sum <- summarise_score(target_dt$pgs_discrete)
  linear_resid_sum <- summarise_score(target_dt$pgs_linear_resid)
  linear_sum <- summarise_score(target_dt$pgs_linear)
  knn_resid_sum <- summarise_score(target_dt$pgs_knn_resid)
  knn_sum <- summarise_score(target_dt$pgs_knn)
  
  summary <- data.table(
    gwas = gwas,
    method = method,
    score_col = score_col,
    n_ref = nrow(ref_dt),
    n_target = nrow(target_dt),
    pc_n = length(pc_cols),
    k_knn = k_knn,
    knn_weighting = weighting,
    
    ref_mean_for_discrete = discrete_fit$ref_mean,
    ref_sd_for_discrete = discrete_fit$ref_sd,
    
    raw_mean = raw_sum$mean,
    raw_sd = raw_sum$sd,
    raw_se = raw_sum$se,
    raw_t = raw_sum$t,
    raw_p = raw_sum$p,
    
    discrete_mean = discrete_sum$mean,
    discrete_sd = discrete_sum$sd,
    discrete_se = discrete_sum$se,
    discrete_t = discrete_sum$t,
    discrete_p = discrete_sum$p,
    
    linear_resid_mean = linear_resid_sum$mean,
    linear_resid_sd = linear_resid_sum$sd,
    linear_resid_se = linear_resid_sum$se,
    linear_resid_t = linear_resid_sum$t,
    linear_resid_p = linear_resid_sum$p,
    
    linear_mean = linear_sum$mean,
    linear_sd = linear_sum$sd,
    linear_se = linear_sum$se,
    linear_t = linear_sum$t,
    linear_p = linear_sum$p,
    
    knn_resid_mean = knn_resid_sum$mean,
    knn_resid_sd = knn_resid_sum$sd,
    knn_resid_se = knn_resid_sum$se,
    knn_resid_t = knn_resid_sum$t,
    knn_resid_p = knn_resid_sum$p,
    
    knn_mean = knn_sum$mean,
    knn_sd = knn_sum$sd,
    knn_se = knn_sum$se,
    knn_t = knn_sum$t,
    knn_p = knn_sum$p,
    
    knn_mean_n_eff = mean(target_dt$knn_n_eff, na.rm = TRUE),
    knn_median_n_eff = median(target_dt$knn_n_eff, na.rm = TRUE),
    knn_mean_dist = mean(target_dt$knn_mean_dist, na.rm = TRUE),
    knn_median_dist = median(target_dt$knn_mean_dist, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    ref_raw_mean = mean(ref_dt$pgs_raw, na.rm = TRUE),
    ref_raw_sd = sd(ref_dt$pgs_raw, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    ref_discrete_mean = mean(ref_dt$pgs_discrete, na.rm = TRUE),
    ref_discrete_sd = sd(ref_dt$pgs_discrete, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    ref_linear_resid_mean = mean(ref_dt$pgs_linear_resid, na.rm = TRUE),
    ref_linear_resid_sd = sd(ref_dt$pgs_linear_resid, na.rm = TRUE),
    ref_linear_mean = mean(ref_dt$pgs_linear, na.rm = TRUE),
    ref_linear_sd = sd(ref_dt$pgs_linear, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    ref_knn_resid_loo_mean = mean(ref_dt$pgs_knn_resid_loo, na.rm = TRUE),
    ref_knn_resid_loo_sd = sd(ref_dt$pgs_knn_resid_loo, na.rm = TRUE),
    ref_knn_loo_mean = mean(ref_dt$pgs_knn_loo, na.rm = TRUE),
    ref_knn_loo_sd = sd(ref_dt$pgs_knn_loo, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    ref_knn_mean_n_eff = mean(ref_dt$knn_n_eff_loo, na.rm = TRUE),
    ref_knn_median_n_eff = median(ref_dt$knn_n_eff_loo, na.rm = TRUE),
    ref_knn_mean_dist = mean(ref_dt$knn_mean_dist_loo, na.rm = TRUE),
    ref_knn_median_dist = median(ref_dt$knn_mean_dist_loo, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
  
  target_dt[, `:=`(
    gwas = gwas,
    method = method,
    score_col = score_col
  )]
  
  ref_dt[, `:=`(
    gwas = gwas,
    method = method,
    score_col = score_col
  )]
  
  return(list(
    summary = summary,
    target_results = target_dt,
    ref_results = ref_dt,
    linear_mean_model = linear_fit$mean_model,
    linear_var_model = linear_fit$var_model
  ))
}

analyse_one_pgs_simple <- function(
    ref_pgs_dt,
    target_pgs_dt,
    ref_pc_dt,
    target_pc_dt,
    gwas,
    method,
    pc_cols = paste0("PC", 1:20),
    k_knn = 50,
    weighting = "inverse_distance",
    pgs_col = NULL
) {
  # Prepare full PGS tables with all score columns
  ref_prep <- prep_pgs_dt(
    ref_pgs_dt,
    gwas = gwas,
    method = method,
    pgs_col = pgs_col
  )
  
  target_prep <- prep_pgs_dt(
    target_pgs_dt,
    gwas = gwas,
    method = method,
    pgs_col = pgs_col
  )
  
  ref_score_cols <- ref_prep$score_cols
  target_score_cols <- target_prep$score_cols
  
  # Use common score columns only
  score_cols <- intersect(ref_score_cols, target_score_cols)
  
  if (length(score_cols) == 0) {
    stop(
      "No shared score columns between reference and target for ",
      gwas, " / ", method,
      "\nReference score columns: ", paste(ref_score_cols, collapse = ", "),
      "\nTarget score columns: ", paste(target_score_cols, collapse = ", ")
    )
  }
  
  # Keep ordering from target
  score_cols <- target_score_cols[target_score_cols %in% score_cols]
  
  results <- list()
  summary_list <- list()
  target_results_list <- list()
  ref_results_list <- list()
  
  for (score_col_i in score_cols) {
    res_i <- analyse_one_score_col_simple(
      ref_score_dt = ref_prep$dt,
      target_score_dt = target_prep$dt,
      ref_pc_dt = ref_pc_dt,
      target_pc_dt = target_pc_dt,
      score_col = score_col_i,
      gwas = gwas,
      method = method,
      pc_cols = pc_cols,
      k_knn = k_knn,
      weighting = weighting
    )
    
    results[[score_col_i]] <- res_i
    summary_list[[score_col_i]] <- res_i$summary
    target_results_list[[score_col_i]] <- res_i$target_results
    ref_results_list[[score_col_i]] <- res_i$ref_results
  }
  
  summary_dt <- rbindlist(summary_list, fill = TRUE)
  
  return(list(
    summary = summary_dt,
    score_results = results,
    target_results = target_results_list,
    ref_results = ref_results_list
  ))
}

###############################################################################
# Wrapper across GWAS and PGS methods
###############################################################################

run_pgs_ancestry_correction_simple <- function(
    ref_pgs_list,
    target_pgs_list,
    target_sample_name,
    ref_pop = "EUR",
    target_pop = "EUR",
    ref_pc_dt,
    target_pc_dt,
    gwas_vec,
    pgs_methods = c("quickprs"),
    pc_cols = paste0("PC", 1:20),
    k_knn = 50,
    weighting = "inverse_distance",
    pgs_col = NULL
) {
  results <- list()
  summary_list <- list()
  
  for (gwas_i in gwas_vec) {
    for (method_i in pgs_methods) {
      
      if (is.null(ref_pgs_list[[ref_pop]])) {
        warning("Reference pop not found in ref_pgs_list: ", ref_pop)
        next
      }
      
      if (is.null(target_pgs_list[[target_sample_name]])) {
        warning("Target sample not found in target_pgs_list: ", target_sample_name)
        next
      }
      
      if (is.null(target_pgs_list[[target_sample_name]][[target_pop]])) {
        warning("Target pop not found: ", target_pop)
        next
      }
      
      if (is.null(ref_pgs_list[[ref_pop]][[gwas_i]])) {
        warning("Reference GWAS not found: ", gwas_i)
        next
      }
      
      if (is.null(target_pgs_list[[target_sample_name]][[target_pop]][[gwas_i]])) {
        warning("Target GWAS not found: ", gwas_i)
        next
      }
      
      if (is.null(ref_pgs_list[[ref_pop]][[gwas_i]][[method_i]])) {
        warning("Reference method not found: ", gwas_i, " / ", method_i)
        next
      }
      
      if (is.null(target_pgs_list[[target_sample_name]][[target_pop]][[gwas_i]][[method_i]])) {
        warning("Target method not found: ", gwas_i, " / ", method_i)
        next
      }
      
      if (is.null(ref_pgs_list[[ref_pop]][[gwas_i]][[method_i]][["raw"]])) {
        warning("Reference raw PGS not found: ", gwas_i, " / ", method_i)
        next
      }
      
      if (is.null(target_pgs_list[[target_sample_name]][[target_pop]][[gwas_i]][[method_i]][["raw"]])) {
        warning("Target raw PGS not found: ", gwas_i, " / ", method_i)
        next
      }
      
      res_i <- analyse_one_pgs_simple(
        ref_pgs_dt = ref_pgs_list[[ref_pop]][[gwas_i]][[method_i]][["raw"]],
        target_pgs_dt = target_pgs_list[[target_sample_name]][[target_pop]][[gwas_i]][[method_i]][["raw"]],
        ref_pc_dt = ref_pc_dt[[ref_pop]],
        target_pc_dt = target_pc_dt[[target_sample_name]][[target_pop]],
        gwas = gwas_i,
        method = method_i,
        pc_cols = pc_cols,
        k_knn = k_knn,
        weighting = weighting,
        pgs_col = pgs_col
      )
      
      key_i <- paste(gwas_i, method_i, sep = "__")
      
      results[[key_i]] <- res_i
      summary_list[[key_i]] <- res_i$summary
    }
  }
  
  summary_dt <- rbindlist(summary_list, fill = TRUE)
  
  return(list(
    summary = summary_dt,
    full_results = results
  ))
}
```

***

### Run code

#### 6 PCs
```{r}
setwd('/users/k1806347/oliverpainfel/Software/MyGit/GenoPred/pipeline/')
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(cowplot)

source('../functions/misc.R')
source_all('../functions')

# Set config
config <- 'misc/opensnp/config_zenodo_10715132.yaml'
gwas_list <- fread('misc/opensnp/gwas_list_zenodo_10715132.txt')

# Target OpenSNP PGS
target_pgs <- read_pgs(
  config = config,
  name = "opensnp"
)

# Reference PGS
ref_pgs <- read_pgs(
  config = config,
  ref = T
)

# Target PCs
target_ref_projected_pc_dt <- read_pcs(config = config, name = 'opensnp', type = 'projected')

# Reference PCs
ref_global_pc_dt<- read_pcs(config = config, ref = T)

res_simple_trans <- run_pgs_ancestry_correction_simple(
  ref_pgs_list = ref_pgs$reference,
  target_pgs_list = target_pgs,
  target_sample_name = "opensnp",
  ref_pop = "TRANS",
  target_pop = "TRANS",
  ref_pc_dt = ref_global_pc_dt$reference,
  target_pc_dt = target_ref_projected_pc_dt,
  gwas_vec = c('yengo_eur', 'yengo_cojo', 'within_sib_height', 'neale_lab_height'),
  pgs_methods = c("quickprs",'ptclump','external'),
  pc_cols = paste0("PC", 1:6),
  k_knn = 50,
  weighting = "inverse_distance"
)

res_simple_eur <- run_pgs_ancestry_correction_simple(
  ref_pgs_list = ref_pgs$reference,
  target_pgs_list = target_pgs,
  target_sample_name = "opensnp",
  ref_pop = "EUR",
  target_pop = "EUR",
  ref_pc_dt = ref_global_pc_dt$reference,
  target_pc_dt = target_ref_projected_pc_dt,
  gwas_vec = c('yengo_eur', 'yengo_cojo', 'within_sib_height', 'neale_lab_height'),
  pgs_methods = c("quickprs",'ptclump','external'),
  pc_cols = paste0("PC", 1:6),
  k_knn = 50,
  weighting = "inverse_distance"
)

res_both <- rbind(
  data.table(res_simple_eur$summary, population = 'EUR'),
  data.table(res_simple_trans$summary, population = 'TRANS'))

res_both$pgs_model <- paste0('pT = ', gsub('.*_0_','',res_both$score_col))
res_both$pgs_model[res_both$method == 'quickprs'] <- 'QuickPRS'
res_both$pgs_model[res_both$method == 'external'] <- 'COJO'
res_both<-res_both[res_both$pgs_model %in% c('pT = 5e-08','QuickPRS','COJO'),]

res_both$gwas[grepl('cojo', res_both$gwas)]<-'yengo_eur'
res_both$gwas <- factor(res_both$gwas, 
                   levels = c("yengo_eur","neale_lab_height","within_sib_height"),
                   labels = c('Yengo','Neale Lab','Howe Within-sib'))

library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Pivot the metrics (mean and sd) for the three correction types
res_long <- res_both %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(discrete_mean, discrete_sd, linear_mean, linear_sd, knn_mean, knn_sd),
    names_to = c("correction", ".value"),
    names_pattern = "(.*)_(mean|sd)"
  ) %>%
  # 2. Reshape again so mean and sd are in one column for faceting
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(mean, sd),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  )

res_long$correction<-factor(res_long$correction, levels = c('discrete','linear','knn'))

# 3. Create the plot
ggplot(res_long, aes(x = gwas, y = value, fill = correction)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), colour = 'black') +
  # Facet rows by Metric (mean/sd) and columns by Population
  # correction is on the x-axis to compare the three methods directly
  facet_grid(metric ~ population + pgs_model, scales = "free", space = 'free_x') +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of PGS Mean and SD across Correction Methods",
    subtitle = "Comparing Raw, Linear, and KNN adjustments",
    x = "GWAS Source",
    y = "Value",
    fill = "Standardisation"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95"),
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines")
  )

ggplot(res_long, aes(x = pgs_model, y = value, fill = gwas)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9, preserve = "single"), colour = 'black') +
  # Facet rows by Metric (mean/sd) and columns by Population
  # correction is on the x-axis to compare the three methods directly
  facet_grid(metric ~ population + correction, scales = "free", space = 'free_x') +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of PGS Mean and SD across Correction Methods",
    subtitle = "Comparing Raw, Linear, and KNN adjustments",
    x = 'Method',
    y = "Value",
    fill = "GWAS Source"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95"),
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines")
  )
########
# Explore KNN results across k
########

###
# TRANS
### 
k_values <- c(10, 20, 50, 100, 200)

res_k <- rbindlist(lapply(k_values, function(k_i) {
  res_i <- run_pgs_ancestry_correction_simple(
    ref_pgs_list = ref_pgs$reference,
    target_pgs_list = target_pgs,
    target_sample_name = "opensnp",
    ref_pop = "TRANS",
    target_pop = "TRANS",
    ref_pc_dt = ref_global_pc_dt$reference,
    target_pc_dt = target_ref_projected_pc_dt,
    gwas_vec = c("neale_lab_height", "within_sib_height", "yengo_eur"),
    pgs_methods = c("ptclump"),
    pc_cols = paste0("PC", 1:6),
    k_knn = k_i,
    weighting = "inverse_distance"
  )
  
  tmp <- copy(res_i$summary)
  tmp[, k_knn := k_i]
  tmp
}), fill = TRUE)

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  res_k[grepl("0_5e-08", res_k$score_col),],
  aes(x = k_knn, y = knn_mean, group = gwas, colour = gwas)
) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  facet_grid(~ gwas, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_classic()

library(FNN)
library(data.table)

pc_cols <- paste0("PC", 1:6)

ref_dt <- ref_global_pc_dt$reference$TRANS
target_dt <- target_ref_projected_pc_dt$opensnp$TRANS

ref_pc <- as.matrix(ref_dt[, ..pc_cols])
target_pc <- as.matrix(target_dt[, ..pc_cols])

k <- 50

target_knn <- FNN::get.knnx(ref_pc, target_pc, k = k)
ref_knn <- FNN::get.knnx(ref_pc, ref_pc, k = k + 1)

target_mean_dist <- rowMeans(target_knn$nn.dist)
ref_mean_dist <- rowMeans(ref_knn$nn.dist[, -1, drop = FALSE])

data.table(
  comparison = c("OpenSNP_to_TRANS_reference", "TRANS_reference_to_TRANS_reference"),
  mean_dist = c(mean(target_mean_dist), mean(ref_mean_dist)),
  median_dist = c(median(target_mean_dist), median(ref_mean_dist)),
  q90 = c(quantile(target_mean_dist, 0.9), quantile(ref_mean_dist, 0.9)),
  q95 = c(quantile(target_mean_dist, 0.95), quantile(ref_mean_dist, 0.95))
)

###
# EUR
###
k_values <- c(10, 20, 50, 100, 200)

res_k <- rbindlist(lapply(k_values, function(k_i) {
  res_i <- run_pgs_ancestry_correction_simple(
    ref_pgs_list = ref_pgs$reference,
    target_pgs_list = target_pgs,
    target_sample_name = "opensnp",
    ref_pop = "EUR",
    target_pop = "EUR",
    ref_pc_dt = ref_global_pc_dt$reference,
    target_pc_dt = target_ref_projected_pc_dt,
    gwas_vec = c("neale_lab_height", "within_sib_height", "yengo_eur"),
    pgs_methods = c("ptclump"),
    pc_cols = paste0("PC", 1:6),
    k_knn = k_i,
    weighting = "inverse_distance"
  )
  
  tmp <- copy(res_i$summary)
  tmp[, k_knn := k_i]
  tmp
}), fill = TRUE)

# Plot result
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  res_k[grepl("0_5e-08", res_k$score_col),],
  aes(x = k_knn, y = knn_mean, group = gwas, colour = gwas)
) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  facet_grid(~ gwas, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_classic()

library(FNN)
library(data.table)

pc_cols <- paste0("PC", 1:6)

ref_dt <- ref_global_pc_dt$reference$EUR
target_dt <- target_ref_projected_pc_dt$opensnp$EUR

ref_pc <- as.matrix(ref_dt[, ..pc_cols])
target_pc <- as.matrix(target_dt[, ..pc_cols])

k <- 50

target_knn <- FNN::get.knnx(ref_pc, target_pc, k = k)
ref_knn <- FNN::get.knnx(ref_pc, ref_pc, k = k + 1)

target_mean_dist <- rowMeans(target_knn$nn.dist)
ref_mean_dist <- rowMeans(ref_knn$nn.dist[, -1, drop = FALSE])

data.table(
  comparison = c("OpenSNP_to_EUR_reference", "EUR_reference_to_EUR_reference"),
  mean_dist = c(mean(target_mean_dist), mean(ref_mean_dist)),
  median_dist = c(median(target_mean_dist), median(ref_mean_dist)),
  q90 = c(quantile(target_mean_dist, 0.9), quantile(ref_mean_dist, 0.9)),
  q95 = c(quantile(target_mean_dist, 0.95), quantile(ref_mean_dist, 0.95))
)

# Seems like ptclump scores with KNN correction in TRANS is the most stable approach

##########
# Run all traits
##########

res_simple_trans <- run_pgs_ancestry_correction_simple(
  ref_pgs_list = ref_pgs$reference,
  target_pgs_list = target_pgs,
  target_sample_name = "opensnp",
  ref_pop = "TRANS",
  target_pop = "TRANS",
  ref_pc_dt = ref_global_pc_dt$reference,
  target_pc_dt = target_ref_projected_pc_dt,
  gwas_vec = gwas_list$name[grepl('neale|within', gwas_list$name)],
  pgs_methods = c('ptclump'),
  pc_cols = paste0("PC", 1:6),
  k_knn = 50,
  weighting = "inverse_distance"
)

res_simple_trans_subset<-res_simple_trans$summary[grepl("0_5e-08", res_simple_trans$summary$score_col),]
res_simple_trans_subset<-res_simple_trans_subset[!grepl('alc', res_simple_trans_subset$gwas),]

# 1. Pivot the metrics (mean and sd) for the three correction types
res_long <- res_simple_trans_subset %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(linear_mean, linear_sd, knn_mean, knn_sd),
    names_to = c("correction", ".value"),
    names_pattern = "(.*)_(mean|sd)"
  ) %>%
  # 2. Reshape again so mean and sd are in one column for faceting
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(mean, sd),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  )

res_long$correction<-factor(res_long$correction, levels = c('linear','knn'))
res_long$gwas_type <- ifelse(grepl('neale', res_long$gwas), 'Unrelated', 'Within-sib')
res_long$trait <- gsub('.*_','',res_long$gwas)

# 3. Create the plot
ggplot(res_long, aes(x = trait, y = value, fill = gwas_type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  # Facet rows by Metric (mean/sd) and columns by Population
  # correction is on the x-axis to compare the three methods directly
  facet_grid(metric ~ correction, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of PGS Mean and SD across Correction Methods",
    subtitle = "Comparing Raw, Linear, and KNN adjustments",
    x = "Trait",
    y = "Value",
    fill = "GWAS Type"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95"),
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines")
  )

# Make a table listing the number of variants in each PGS
score_file_list <- list_score_files('misc/opensnp/config_zenodo_10715132.yaml')
score_file_list <- score_file_list[grepl('within|neale', score_file_list$name),]

n_snp <- NULL
for(i in 1:nrow(score_file_list)){
  tmp <- fread(paste0('~/oliverpainfel/Data/OpenSNP/GenoPred/zenodo_10715132/reference/pgs_score_files/',score_file_list$method[i],'/',score_file_list$name[i],'/ref-',score_file_list$name[i],'.score.gz'))
  n_snp_i <- apply(tmp[,-1:-3, with = F], 2, function(x) sum(x != 0))
  n_snp <- rbind(
    n_snp,
    data.table(
      name = score_file_list$name[i],
      method = score_file_list$method[i],
      param = names(n_snp_i),
      n_snp = n_snp_i
    )
  )
}

n_snp[grepl('5e-08', param),]

```

#### 40 PCs
```{r}
setwd('/users/k1806347/oliverpainfel/Software/MyGit/GenoPred/pipeline/')
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(cowplot)

source('../functions/misc.R')
source_all('../functions')

# Set config
config <- 'misc/opensnp/config_zenodo_10715132.20_ref_pcs.yaml'
gwas_list <- fread('misc/opensnp/gwas_list_zenodo_10715132.txt')

# Target OpenSNP PGS
target_pgs <- read_pgs(
  config = config,
  name = "opensnp"
)

# Reference PGS
ref_pgs <- read_pgs(
  config = config,
  ref = T
)

# Target PCs
target_ref_projected_pc_dt <- read_pcs(config = config, name = 'opensnp', type = 'projected')

# Reference PCs
ref_global_pc_dt<- read_pcs(config = config, ref = T)

res_simple_trans <- run_pgs_ancestry_correction_simple(
  ref_pgs_list = ref_pgs$reference,
  target_pgs_list = target_pgs,
  target_sample_name = "opensnp",
  ref_pop = "TRANS",
  target_pop = "TRANS",
  ref_pc_dt = ref_global_pc_dt$reference,
  target_pc_dt = target_ref_projected_pc_dt,
  gwas_vec = c('yengo_eur', 'yengo_cojo', 'within_sib_height', 'neale_lab_height'),
  pgs_methods = c("quickprs",'ptclump','external'),
  pc_cols = paste0("PC", 1:40),
  k_knn = 50,
  weighting = "inverse_distance"
)

res_simple_eur <- run_pgs_ancestry_correction_simple(
  ref_pgs_list = ref_pgs$reference,
  target_pgs_list = target_pgs,
  target_sample_name = "opensnp",
  ref_pop = "EUR",
  target_pop = "EUR",
  ref_pc_dt = ref_global_pc_dt$reference,
  target_pc_dt = target_ref_projected_pc_dt,
  gwas_vec = c('yengo_eur', 'yengo_cojo', 'within_sib_height', 'neale_lab_height'),
  pgs_methods = c("quickprs",'ptclump','external'),
  pc_cols = paste0("PC", 1:40),
  k_knn = 50,
  weighting = "inverse_distance"
)

res_both <- rbind(
  data.table(res_simple_eur$summary, population = 'EUR'),
  data.table(res_simple_trans$summary, population = 'TRANS'))

res_both$pgs_model <- paste0('pT = ', gsub('.*_0_','',res_both$score_col))
res_both$pgs_model[res_both$method == 'quickprs'] <- 'QuickPRS'
res_both$pgs_model[res_both$method == 'external'] <- 'COJO'
res_both<-res_both[res_both$pgs_model %in% c('pT = 5e-08','QuickPRS','COJO'),]

res_both$gwas[grepl('cojo', res_both$gwas)]<-'yengo_eur'
res_both$gwas <- factor(res_both$gwas, 
                   levels = c("yengo_eur","neale_lab_height","within_sib_height"),
                   labels = c('Yengo','Neale Lab','Howe Within-sib'))

library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Pivot the metrics (mean and sd) for the three correction types
res_long <- res_both %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(discrete_mean, discrete_sd, linear_mean, linear_sd, knn_mean, knn_sd),
    names_to = c("correction", ".value"),
    names_pattern = "(.*)_(mean|sd)"
  ) %>%
  # 2. Reshape again so mean and sd are in one column for faceting
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(mean, sd),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  )

res_long$correction<-factor(res_long$correction, levels = c('discrete','linear','knn'))

# 3. Create the plot
ggplot(res_long, aes(x = gwas, y = value, fill = correction)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), colour = 'black') +
  # Facet rows by Metric (mean/sd) and columns by Population
  # correction is on the x-axis to compare the three methods directly
  facet_grid(metric ~ population + pgs_model, scales = "free", space = 'free_x') +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of PGS Mean and SD across Correction Methods",
    subtitle = "Comparing Raw, Linear, and KNN adjustments",
    x = "GWAS Source",
    y = "Value",
    fill = "Standardisation"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95"),
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines")
  )

ggplot(res_long, aes(x = pgs_model, y = value, fill = gwas)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9, preserve = "single"), colour = 'black') +
  # Facet rows by Metric (mean/sd) and columns by Population
  # correction is on the x-axis to compare the three methods directly
  facet_grid(metric ~ population + correction, scales = "free", space = 'free_x') +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of PGS Mean and SD across Correction Methods",
    subtitle = "Comparing Raw, Linear, and KNN adjustments",
    x = 'Method',
    y = "Value",
    fill = "GWAS Source"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95"),
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines")
  )
########
# Explore KNN results across k
########

###
# TRANS
### 
k_values <- c(10, 20, 50, 100, 200)

res_k <- rbindlist(lapply(k_values, function(k_i) {
  res_i <- run_pgs_ancestry_correction_simple(
    ref_pgs_list = ref_pgs$reference,
    target_pgs_list = target_pgs,
    target_sample_name = "opensnp",
    ref_pop = "TRANS",
    target_pop = "TRANS",
    ref_pc_dt = ref_global_pc_dt$reference,
    target_pc_dt = target_ref_projected_pc_dt,
    gwas_vec = c("neale_lab_height", "within_sib_height", "yengo_eur"),
    pgs_methods = c("ptclump"),
    pc_cols = paste0("PC", 1:6),
    k_knn = k_i,
    weighting = "inverse_distance"
  )
  
  tmp <- copy(res_i$summary)
  tmp[, k_knn := k_i]
  tmp
}), fill = TRUE)

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  res_k[grepl("0_5e-08", res_k$score_col),],
  aes(x = k_knn, y = knn_mean, group = gwas, colour = gwas)
) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  facet_grid(~ gwas, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_classic()

library(FNN)
library(data.table)

pc_cols <- paste0("PC", 1:6)

ref_dt <- ref_global_pc_dt$reference$TRANS
target_dt <- target_ref_projected_pc_dt$opensnp$TRANS

ref_pc <- as.matrix(ref_dt[, ..pc_cols])
target_pc <- as.matrix(target_dt[, ..pc_cols])

k <- 50

target_knn <- FNN::get.knnx(ref_pc, target_pc, k = k)
ref_knn <- FNN::get.knnx(ref_pc, ref_pc, k = k + 1)

target_mean_dist <- rowMeans(target_knn$nn.dist)
ref_mean_dist <- rowMeans(ref_knn$nn.dist[, -1, drop = FALSE])

data.table(
  comparison = c("OpenSNP_to_TRANS_reference", "TRANS_reference_to_TRANS_reference"),
  mean_dist = c(mean(target_mean_dist), mean(ref_mean_dist)),
  median_dist = c(median(target_mean_dist), median(ref_mean_dist)),
  q90 = c(quantile(target_mean_dist, 0.9), quantile(ref_mean_dist, 0.9)),
  q95 = c(quantile(target_mean_dist, 0.95), quantile(ref_mean_dist, 0.95))
)

###
# EUR
###
k_values <- c(10, 20, 50, 100, 200)

res_k <- rbindlist(lapply(k_values, function(k_i) {
  res_i <- run_pgs_ancestry_correction_simple(
    ref_pgs_list = ref_pgs$reference,
    target_pgs_list = target_pgs,
    target_sample_name = "opensnp",
    ref_pop = "EUR",
    target_pop = "EUR",
    ref_pc_dt = ref_global_pc_dt$reference,
    target_pc_dt = target_ref_projected_pc_dt,
    gwas_vec = c("neale_lab_height", "within_sib_height", "yengo_eur"),
    pgs_methods = c("ptclump"),
    pc_cols = paste0("PC", 1:6),
    k_knn = k_i,
    weighting = "inverse_distance"
  )
  
  tmp <- copy(res_i$summary)
  tmp[, k_knn := k_i]
  tmp
}), fill = TRUE)

# Plot result
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  res_k[grepl("0_5e-08", res_k$score_col),],
  aes(x = k_knn, y = knn_mean, group = gwas, colour = gwas)
) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  facet_grid(~ gwas, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_classic()

library(FNN)
library(data.table)

pc_cols <- paste0("PC", 1:6)

ref_dt <- ref_global_pc_dt$reference$EUR
target_dt <- target_ref_projected_pc_dt$opensnp$EUR

ref_pc <- as.matrix(ref_dt[, ..pc_cols])
target_pc <- as.matrix(target_dt[, ..pc_cols])

k <- 50

target_knn <- FNN::get.knnx(ref_pc, target_pc, k = k)
ref_knn <- FNN::get.knnx(ref_pc, ref_pc, k = k + 1)

target_mean_dist <- rowMeans(target_knn$nn.dist)
ref_mean_dist <- rowMeans(ref_knn$nn.dist[, -1, drop = FALSE])

data.table(
  comparison = c("OpenSNP_to_EUR_reference", "EUR_reference_to_EUR_reference"),
  mean_dist = c(mean(target_mean_dist), mean(ref_mean_dist)),
  median_dist = c(median(target_mean_dist), median(ref_mean_dist)),
  q90 = c(quantile(target_mean_dist, 0.9), quantile(ref_mean_dist, 0.9)),
  q95 = c(quantile(target_mean_dist, 0.95), quantile(ref_mean_dist, 0.95))
)

# Seems like ptclump scores with KNN correction in TRANS is the most stable approach

##########
# Run all traits
##########

res_simple_trans <- run_pgs_ancestry_correction_simple(
  ref_pgs_list = ref_pgs$reference,
  target_pgs_list = target_pgs,
  target_sample_name = "opensnp",
  ref_pop = "TRANS",
  target_pop = "TRANS",
  ref_pc_dt = ref_global_pc_dt$reference,
  target_pc_dt = target_ref_projected_pc_dt,
  gwas_vec = gwas_list$name[grepl('neale|within', gwas_list$name)],
  pgs_methods = c('ptclump'),
  pc_cols = paste0("PC", 1:6),
  k_knn = 50,
  weighting = "inverse_distance"
)

res_simple_trans_subset<-res_simple_trans$summary[grepl("0_5e-08", res_simple_trans$summary$score_col),]
res_simple_trans_subset<-res_simple_trans_subset[!grepl('alc', res_simple_trans_subset$gwas),]

# 1. Pivot the metrics (mean and sd) for the three correction types
res_long <- res_simple_trans_subset %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(linear_mean, linear_sd, knn_mean, knn_sd),
    names_to = c("correction", ".value"),
    names_pattern = "(.*)_(mean|sd)"
  ) %>%
  # 2. Reshape again so mean and sd are in one column for faceting
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(mean, sd),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  )

res_long$correction<-factor(res_long$correction, levels = c('linear','knn'))
res_long$gwas_type <- ifelse(grepl('neale', res_long$gwas), 'Unrelated', 'Within-sib')
res_long$trait <- gsub('.*_','',res_long$gwas)

# 3. Create the plot
ggplot(res_long, aes(x = trait, y = value, fill = gwas_type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  # Facet rows by Metric (mean/sd) and columns by Population
  # correction is on the x-axis to compare the three methods directly
  facet_grid(metric ~ correction, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of PGS Mean and SD across Correction Methods",
    subtitle = "Comparing Raw, Linear, and KNN adjustments",
    x = "Trait",
    y = "Value",
    fill = "GWAS Type"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95"),
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines")
  )

# Make a table listing the number of variants in each PGS
score_file_list <- list_score_files('misc/opensnp/config_zenodo_10715132.yaml')
score_file_list <- score_file_list[grepl('within|neale', score_file_list$name),]

n_snp <- NULL
for(i in 1:nrow(score_file_list)){
  tmp <- fread(paste0('~/oliverpainfel/Data/OpenSNP/GenoPred/zenodo_10715132/reference/pgs_score_files/',score_file_list$method[i],'/',score_file_list$name[i],'/ref-',score_file_list$name[i],'.score.gz'))
  n_snp_i <- apply(tmp[,-1:-3, with = F], 2, function(x) sum(x != 0))
  n_snp <- rbind(
    n_snp,
    data.table(
      name = score_file_list$name[i],
      method = score_file_list$method[i],
      param = names(n_snp_i),
      n_snp = n_snp_i
    )
  )
}

n_snp[grepl('5e-08', param),]

```
